task_completion.description: Agent opens and analyzes the baseline_audit.pcapng file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Wireshark open with baseline_audit.pcapng loaded, or terminal commands like 'tshark -r baseline_audit.pcapng' or 'capinfos' being executed.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the baseline_audit_report.txt file in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (nano, vim, gedit) is open editing '/home/ga/Documents/captures/baseline_audit_report.txt', or terminal commands show the file being created/written to this exact path.

task_completion.description: The report includes a clearly labeled section for all unique protocols observed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains a heading like 'Unique Protocols' and lists the 10 protocols (eth, ethertype, ip, tcp, udp, http, dns, data, data-text-lines, xml).

task_completion.description: The report includes a clearly labeled section for all unique source and destination IP addresses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains a heading for 'Unique IP Addresses' (or similar) and lists exactly 10 distinct IP addresses.

task_completion.description: The report includes a clearly labeled section for all unique destination TCP/UDP ports.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains a heading for 'Unique Destination Ports' and lists exactly 16 distinct port numbers.

task_completion.description: The report includes the count of TCP retransmissions, total packets, and total bytes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report explicitly states '1 TCP retransmission', '116 packets', and '40,320 bytes' (or very similar phrasing).

integrity.description: Agent extracted the data using actual network analysis tools rather than guessing or hallucinating.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of Wireshark GUI interactions, tshark/tcpdump commands, or Python scapy scripts that were actually executed to parse the PCAP file.

integrity.description: The report was saved to the filesystem, not just displayed on screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: Commands like 'cat baseline_audit_report.txt', 'ls -l', or a text editor showing the file is saved at the correct path without unsaved changes indicators.

integrity.description: The agent did not hallucinate generic network data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the report exactly match the privileged info (116 packets, 40320 bytes, 1 retransmission) rather than generic placeholder examples like '1000 packets'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The baseline_audit.pcapng file contains exactly 116 packets and 40,320 bytes. It has 10 unique protocols (eth, ethertype, ip, tcp, udp, http, dns, data, data-text-lines, xml), 10 unique IP endpoints, 16 unique destination ports, and exactly 1 TCP retransmission. The final report must be located at /home/ga/Documents/captures/baseline_audit_report.txt.
